summary: McKenzie provides a brief progress update on his Kalzumeus side project, noting it has reached 25% feature completion but is far from being user-ready. The interface exists as functional skeletons but needs significant polish before public exposure. He plans to reveal the project publicly at 50% completion, potentially within a week. He mentions spending Japan's Golden Week holiday coding and writing resumes rather than vacationing.
key_points: Feature completeness and user-readiness are different metrics — having the right form elements doesn't mean the product is ready for users
McKenzie planned to go public with the project concept at 50% completion rather than waiting for a full launch
He was balancing side project development with job searching (writing resumes) during his holiday time
money_quotes: Its certainly 25% feature complete but its not 25% of the way ready to actually have users.
I have functional skeletons up for most of it but they are too ugly to ever show to people, even if they do have the right form elements on them.
Its Golden Week and this year instead of taking a well-deserved vacation I'll be coding and writing resumes, bleh.
key_insight: Feature completion percentage is misleading because having functional code is only a fraction of the work needed to make software ready for actual users.
